title: Citronella bug-repellent balm
subtitle: A beeswax and coconut-oil balm with citronella and eucalyptus for outdoor insect deterrence
excerpt: Apply to pulse points and exposed skin before going outdoors. Citronella and eucalyptus essential oils have documented insect-deterrent activity; beeswax and coconut oil give the formula a slow-release base that extends contact time on the skin.
sourceNotes: Citronella oil insect repellent activity: Fradin, New England Journal of Medicine (1998). Eucalyptus oil repellent properties: DEET-free alternatives reviewed in Maia and Moore, Malaria Journal (2011). Beeswax as a slow-release carrier: cosmetic formulation reference.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep each tin to 30 ml for correct essential-oil concentration per application.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The balm keeps for a year. Make a batch at the start of summer.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Use within one year. Discard if the balm develops a rancid smell or if the surface feels greasy and separated.

glossaryTerms.definition: Steam-distilled essential oil from Cymbopogon nardus grass. Contains citronellal and geraniol compounds that are repellent to mosquitoes and biting flies. Effective for light deterrence at 5 to 10% concentration.

content.text: alongside eucalyptus, both of which have documented repellent activity against mosquitoes. The beeswax and coconut-oil base creates a solid balm that melts on skin contact and clings for longer than a water-based spray. Apply generously before going outdoors and reapply every two to three hours.
Place the beeswax pellets and coconut oil in a heatproof container over a double boiler. Melt over a gentle heat, stirring occasionally, until fully liquid. Remove from the heat.
Allow the melted base to cool slightly to about 50 to 55°C -- too hot and the essential oils will flash off. Add the citronella, eucalyptus, and lavender drops and stir through.
Pour into two small tins or lip-balm-style containers. Leave undisturbed at room temperature for 30 minutes until fully set. Cap when completely cool.

Rub a small amount of balm on wrists, ankles, the back of the neck, and any exposed skin. Reapply every two to three hours outdoors. Not suitable for children under 2 years -- do not apply to the face or hands of young children. Keep away from eyes. Do not apply to cats or dogs.
Keep at room temperature in the sealed tin. Use within one year. Discard if the balm develops a rancid smell or if the surface is greasy and oily.
